diagrams.title: Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows contiguous memory allocation for a 5-element integer array with base address 1000, where each int occupies 4 bytes
elements: Address calculation: addr[i] = base + i × size
diagrams.purpose: Understanding contiguous storage and O(1) access

diagrams.title: Structure vs Union Memory
diagrams.description: Compares memory allocation for a struct and union both containing int, float, and char members
elements: Structure: int (4 bytes) + float (4 bytes) + char (1 byte) = 9 bytes (typically padded to 12)
Union: single memory block of 4 bytes (largest member)
Structure has separate storage for each member
Union shares storage among all members
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing memory sharing concept in unions

diagrams.title: Sparse Matrix Representation (COO Format)
diagrams.description: Shows a 4×4 sparse matrix with only 3 non-zero elements and how they're stored in COO format
elements: Original 4×4 matrix with zeros and few non-zeros
Three arrays: row indices, column indices, values
Example: element 5 at position (0,3) stored as row[0]=0, col[0]=3, val[0]=5
Space saved: 16 elements vs 3 non-zero elements stored

diagrams.title: Row-Major vs Column-Major Storage
diagrams.description: Shows a 3×3 matrix stored in both row-major and column-major order with element positions
elements: Row-major sequence: a00, a01, a02, a10, a11, a12, a20, a21, a22
Column-major sequence: a00, a10, a20, a01, a11, a21, a02, a12, a22
